name: Dar-us-Sakina
mission: DAR-US-SAKINA IS A 501(C)(3) NONPROFIT ORGANIZATION DEDICATED TO INCLUSION OF INDIVIDUALS WITH DISABILITIES IN THE MUSLIM COMMUNITY OF HOUSTON. OUR PLATFORM IS A SAFE SPACE FOR PEOPLE OF ALL ABILITIES AND THEIR CAREGIVERS. WE INSPIRE THE COMMUNITY TO ABANDON STIGMAS AND EMBRACE INDIVIDUALS WITH DISABILITIES WITH EMPATHY AND RESPECT. TOGETHER, WE ARE BETTER.
scoreSummary: Too early to rate numerically. This organization was founded in 2021 and is shown as an emerging organization while it builds a public track record.

evaluationSources: Support for Community Awareness Programs (Islamic Relief USA)

zakatClaimEvidence: Dar-us-Sakina explicitly states: "A large proportion of our programming is Zakat-eligible, allowing your donation to directly uplift individuals and families facing ongoing financial hardship due to disability." They also mention reserving "its Zakat fund for Muslim families who are in financial need or crisis." Furthermore, they have a "Zakat Program" and "Zakat Fund" to provide comprehensive support for special needs families in crisis, including emergency respite care, counseling, and durable medical equipment. On donation platforms, 'Zakat' is listed as a campaign option. (Source: https://www.every.org/dar-us-sakina)

ui_signals_v1.recommendation_rationale: Early-stage organization. We show qualitative context while it builds a longer public track record.

components.evidence: Explicit zakat program (+4); Muslim-focused organization (+2); Islamic identity (+1); Asnaf: masakin (+5); Strong humanitarian service (+4)

baseline_narrative.amal_score_rationale: The organization earns a moderate overall score reflecting its strong financial health and niche community alignment, balanced by a need for more robust external impact verification.

Opportunity to increase the program expense ratio to further direct more resources to services

dimension_explanations.alignment: With an alignment score of 31/50, the charity shows high donor fit for those seeking to support religious and cultural causes specifically tailored to the special needs community [8]. Its zakat-eligible status and adherence to specific asnaf categories further strengthen its appeal to Muslim donors [2][10].
dimension_explanations.impact: The organization focuses on capacity building through specialized educational hours and family support via its Zakat Fund [9]. While it maintains a solid Charity Navigator score of 93.0/100 and a program expense ratio of 72.2%, the impact score of 29/50 reflects a medium level of data confidence regarding cost-per-beneficiary metrics [1][3].

baseline_narrative.headline: Dar-us-Sakina empowers individuals with disabilities in the Houston Muslim community through inclusive education and specialized support services.
strengths: High financial efficiency with a fundraising efficiency of $0.06 per $1 raised
Strong liquidity with 15.9 months of working capital
Diverse programming including Sunday school, internships, and volunteer enrichment
baseline_narrative.summary: Dar-us-Sakina provides inclusive opportunities and empowerment for individuals with disabilities within the Muslim community of Houston [4][5]. The organization maintains a strong financial standing with a total revenue of $1,115,693 and is recognized as zakat-eligible [1][2].

ideal_donor_profile.best_for_summary: Best for donors who want to support local, faith-based disability inclusion and fulfill zakat through specialized community infrastructure.
donor_motivations: Want to address the underserved need for disability support in the Muslim community
Value high financial transparency and low fundraising overhead
Seek zakat-eligible opportunities with clear religious backing
giving_considerations: The organization is in a growth stage with a 24.6% revenue CAGR
Impact is measured through individual student progress rather than large-scale RCTs
Donations support a highly specialized, niche service area
ideal_donor_profile.not_ideal_for: Donors seeking large-scale international humanitarian relief or those who require third-party academic impact evaluations.

strengths.detail: The organization operates with a <cite id="1">fundraising efficiency of $0.06 per $1 raised</cite>, ensuring that the vast majority of contributions support core services.
Dar-us-Sakina maintains <cite id="1">15.9 months of working capital</cite>, providing a significant buffer for long-term operational sustainability.
The charity has <cite id="8">formal fatwa documentation</cite> for its programs and ensures <cite id="2">zakat eligibility for its beneficiaries</cite>.

strengths_deep_dive: Dar-us-Sakina's operational model is characterized by high transparency and governance standards, evidenced by a <cite id="1">72.2% independent board of directors</cite>. Financially, the organization is in a growth phase, with <cite id="3">net assets increasing to $462,771</cite> by the end of 2023. Their program delivery is highly direct, utilizing <cite id="11">volunteer enrichment programs</cite> and specialized staff to manage <cite id="9">student progress reports and goal tracking</cite> for approximately 100 individuals. This structured approach to disability services within a religious context is a rare and vital resource for the Houston Muslim community.
rich_narrative.summary: Dar-us-Sakina is a specialized Houston-based nonprofit dedicated to providing <cite id="4">inclusive opportunities for individuals with disabilities</cite> within the Muslim community. The organization addresses a critical gap in community services by offering <cite id="8">specialized educational and support programs</cite> tailored to the unique needs of families. In the 2023 fiscal year, the charity reported <cite id="3">total revenue of $540,354</cite>, demonstrating a significant <cite id="3">3-year revenue CAGR of 24.6%</cite>. This growth supports a diverse service portfolio, including the <cite id="11">Sakina Sunday School, Adult Day Programs, and caregiver training</cite>. The organization maintains a <cite id="1">93/100 overall score from Charity Navigator</cite>, reflecting a high standard of <cite id="1">accountability and leadership</cite>. Furthermore, Dar-us-Sakina is <cite id="2">verified as zakat-eligible</cite>, specifically serving the <cite id="2">fuqara (poor) and masakin (needy)</cite> categories of asnaf. By operating across <cite id="1">two active masjid sites</cite>, the charity ensures that disability support is integrated directly into the spiritual and social fabric of the local community.
